questions.question: If the takeoff roll distance of an airplane is 283 yards, and the distance to clear a 50 foot obstacle is 145% of the takeoff roll, what is the total takeoff distance required to clear the obstacle?
If the previous takeoff roll distance is to be reduced by 28 feet for each knot of headwind component, the winds are 330@15 mph, and the takeoff runway is 26, what is the wind-corrected takeoff roll?
questions.context: Referenced question 1: If the takeoff roll distance of an airplane is 283 yards, and the distance to clear a 50 foot obstacle is 145% of the takeoff roll, what is the total takeoff distance required to clear the obstacle?

questions.question: After that takeoff, the average rate of climb is 1680 fpm for 200 feet and 1120 fpm for the rest of the climb. How long will it take to climb from the airport elevation of 985 feet to 3500 feet MSL?
Assuming the wind was constant during the test flight, what was the wind?
questions.context: You do not know what the correction is from IAS to CAS on a new airplane. You fly at a constant indicated 55 knots on two reciprocal headings, 180 and 360, to collect data. The pressure altitude is 3655 and the OAT is 20°F. The southbound run lasts 67 seconds and covers 1.4 nautical miles. The northbound run lasts 112 seconds and covers the same 1.4 nautical miles. Use this information to answer the next four questions.

questions.question: Keeping in mind that the IAS is suspected to be unreliable, what was the TAS?
questions.context: You do not know what the correction is from IAS to CAS on a new airplane. You fly at a constant indicated 55 knots on two reciprocal headings, 180 and 360, to collect data. The pressure altitude is 3655 and the OAT is 20°F. The southbound run lasts 67 seconds and covers 1.4 nautical miles. The northbound run lasts 112 seconds and covers the same 1.4 nautical miles. questions.question: What was the density altitude at the time of the test?

questions.question: You repeat the test using an indicated speed of 50 knots under the same atmospheric conditions. In this second case, you calculate that your true airspeed is 55 knots. Find your CAS and determine that in this condition, the airspeed indicator's presentation is:
questions.context: You do not know what the correction is from IAS to CAS on a new airplane. You fly at a constant indicated 55 knots on two reciprocal headings, 180 and 360, to collect data. The pressure altitude is 3655 and the OAT is 20°F. The southbound run lasts 67 seconds and covers 1.4 nautical miles. The northbound run lasts 112 seconds and covers the same 1.4 nautical miles. questions.question: You fly a heading of 320 and drift 6? right. Then you fly a heading of 260 and drift 3? left. Your TAS is 155 knots. What is the wind direction and speed?
You cross 8° of radials from a VOR in 4.5 minutes. How long will it take for you to fly directly to the VOR?

Reading your altitude of 500' (with 30.01" altimeter setting) on final you realize you forgot to set the current local altimeter setting, 30.24". What is your actual altitude AGL if the elevation is 133'?
What is your TAS?
questions.context: Use the following data for questions 11-14: [[NEXT_LINE]] TC: 170 outbound Pressure altitude: 5,000' IAS: 140 knots. [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds: 200 @ 25 knots OAT: 0 C Fuel: 40 US gal Burn: 9.5 gph.

questions.question: What is your radius of action? (Assuming zero fuel reserves)
questions.context: Use the following data for questions 11-14: [[NEXT_LINE]] TC: 170 outbound Pressure altitude: 5,000' IAS: 140 knots. [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds: 200 @ 25 knots OAT: 0 C Fuel: 40 US gal Burn: 9.5 gph.

questions.question: What is your total trip time if you flew the radius of action?

questions.question: If you wanted to fly straight on the outbound heading until you ran out of fuel, how far could you travel?
questions.context: Use the following data for questions 11-14: [[NEXT_LINE]] TC: 170 outbound Pressure altitude: 5,000' IAS: 140 knots. [[NEXT_LINE]] Winds: 200 @ 25 knots OAT: 0 C Fuel: 40 US gal Burn: 9.5 gph.

questions.question: How much does 7 quarts of oil weigh?

While fueling in a foreign land, you know you need 255,000 lb. of fuel in your Boeing 747-400. You already know how many gallons of fuel you need, since that's an easy 6.75 lb./gallon, but the fueler asks how many liters of fuel you need.

After completing your weight and balance, you find that your gross weight is 910 kg., and you are 1.5" aft of your center of gravity. How many 20 lb. boxes of cargo will you have to move to the next cargo hold 20" forward to come within limits?

The departure procedure off of Rwy 30L in Dubai states you must maintain a climb gradient of 305 feet/nm to 2,000' MSL. Your climbout speed today is 162 KIAS. The winds are calm at this sea level airport with an OAT of 115°F. What vertical speed must you maintain to 2,000' MSL to comply with this departure procedure?
You are departing runway 25R and the winds are 300° at 24 knots. Does this exceed the maximum demonstrated crosswind limitation of 15 knots?

choices.text: No. It is right at the limitation.
No. It is four knots under the limitation.
Yes. You exceed the limitation by three knots.
Yes. You exceed the limitation by eight knots.

questions.correctChoice: Yes. You exceed the limitation by three knots.

questions.question: A pilot inbound on the 360° radial of a VOR turns 20° left and flies for 8 minutes. When the pilot crosses the 020° radial they turn inbound again. How long will it take to fly to the VOR?

An air traffic controller sees two aircraft on his radar scope flying the same jet route at the same altitude 5 NM apart. He has 0:16:40 to get 30 NM separation between the two aircraft. The first jet is told to maintain max forward speed, which the controller sees from his scope is 13 NM every 2 minutes. Winds at altitude are a 60 knot headwind and temperature is -42°C. What maximum speed can the controller tell the second, trailing plane to fly in order to get 30 NM separation? Assume the time and speed for the second plane to slow down is negligible.
Find cruise ground speed at FL290 if winds aloft are 255° at 141 knots, indicated airspeed is 207 knots, outside air temperature is ISA-8°C, and magnetic course is 093°.
325 milliliters = ____ ounces
You are doing a turn around a point and you are maintaining 90 knots and in one full circle you travel 2.6 nm. How far from the point are you maintaining on average?
Referencing the previous question, how long will it take you to do four full turns around a point?
questions.context: Referenced question 24: You are doing a turn around a point and you are maintaining 90 knots and in one full circle you travel 2.6 nm. How far from the point are you maintaining on average?

questions.question: You are going 234 KTAS at 14,000' and the temperature is -22°C, what percentage of the speed of sound are you going?
Referencing the previous question, what is your speed in KIAS?
questions.context: Referenced question 26: You are going 234 KTAS at 14,000' and the temperature is -22°C, what percentage of the speed of sound are you going?

questions.question: You are transporting a drum with 48 gallons of AVGAS to northern Alaska. The drum sits on a pallet measuring 24 inches square. Your aircraft has a floor load limit of 80 lbs/square feet. Can you load the barrel with the pallet it rests on now and remain within the limitation? (disregard the weight of the drum and pallet itself)

choices.text: No, the pallet needs to be 0.6 square feet larger
Yes, the pallet is large enough by 0.4 square feet
Yes, the pallet is large enough by 0.8 square feet
No, the pallet needs to be 8 square feet larger

questions.correctChoice: Yes, the pallet is large enough by 0.4 square feet

questions.question: Regardless, you load the drum of AVGAS anyways, at an average station of 128". The CG before the AVGAS was loaded was 97". The new weight of the aircraft is 2950 lbs. What is the new CG?

Your best friend just got tickets for the both of you to see the Green Bay Packers take on the Minnesota Vikings in Green Bay. He asks that you fly from Green Bay to pick him up from Traverse City Airport in your spacious Cessna 152. If you want to arrive back in Green Bay 2 hours before kickoff, when is the latest you should take off from Green Bay? (Kick off is 15:30 CST; disregard time spent on the ground in TVC)
questions.context: Use the following info for questions 30-32: [[NEXT_LINE]] Dist. = 110 nm Magnetic Course (GRB-TVC) = 083 TAS = 121 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] Magnetic Winds = 310@35 knots Fuel Flow = 5.5 gph [[NEXT_LINE]] Assume the same performance for climb and descent.

questions.question: On the way back, 52 sm from GRB, your friend is starting to experience chest tightness. At this point would it be quicker to continue to GRB, or return to TVC?

choices.text: Continue to GRB, you passed your equal time point 13 nm ago
Return to TVC, you're short of your equal time point by 3 nm
Return to TVC, you're short of your equal time point by 5 nm
Return to TVC, you're short of your equal time point by 7 nm

questions.correctChoice: Return to TVC, you're short of your equal time point by 3 nm
questions.context: Use the following info for questions 30-32: [[NEXT_LINE]] Dist. = 110 nm Magnetic Course (GRB-TVC) = 083 TAS = 121 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] Magnetic Winds = 310@35 knots Fuel Flow = 5.5 gph [[NEXT_LINE]] Assume the same performance for climb and descent.

questions.question: Your friend turns out to be alright though, and you arrive in GRB without incident. If fuel costs $5.74 per gallon, how much did you spend on fuel for your flight today?
questions.context: Use the following info for questions 30-32: [[NEXT_LINE]] Dist. = 110 nm Magnetic Course (GRB-TVC) = 083 TAS = 121 mph [[NEXT_LINE]] Magnetic Winds = 310@35 knots Fuel Flow = 5.5 gph [[NEXT_LINE]] Assume the same performance for climb and descent.

questions.question: The O'Hare Six departure requires you to cross the 5 DME arc from the VOR at 3,000' MSL. Your planned runway for departure will have you rotate on the 1 DME arc from the VOR as you climb away from the station. Airport elevation is 650'. Assuming a straight out departure, what climb rate must you maintain if your average ground speed is 200 knots?
The Polar Vortex is Back! At your local field, the elevation is 860', the OAT is -26 Celsius, and the local altimeter setting is 30.56" Hg. What is the density altitude at the field?
Curious as to the coldest place on Earth now, you learn that scientists recently measured a record setting -93° Celsius in Antarctica. What is that in Fahrenheit?
Cruising at FL200, the OAT is -34 Celsius, and your CAS is 270 knots. What is your TAS?
An F-16 is maintaining 200 knots true airspeed in a straight vertical climb. What rate is it climbing at in feet per second?

You are out for a short afternoon practice flight for the VFR Navigation event. Unfortunately, the government seems to be testing GPS jamming equipment today and you have lost the magenta line as your GPS failed. You find yourself reverting to pilotage once again, and discover you have drifted 1.5 nautical miles left of your course. If you discover this 12.1 nautical miles into a 23.3 nautical mile leg, how many degrees must you turn to arrive over the checkpoint?
While planning for this practice VFR Navigation flight, you figure your time to fly 1 statute mile to be 33 seconds. What is your indicated airspeed on a calm wind, standard day at 4,500' MSL?
The wind is forecast to be from 225° at 28 knots for your cross country flight. If your true course is 133° and you plan a true airspeed of 144 mph, what is your ground speed?
